info.title: Topos product API (device lane)
info.description: The session lane the product app serves: the RFC-8628-shaped login flow (start/poll — approval promotes the flow code to the SESSION's workspace-scoped bearer credential), the session self-end, the publish/propose/revert/review writes, the current/version/object/catalog/proposals reads, the delivery + applied-state report, the describe reads, and the row ops (the server-stored profile / channel curation / protection / notices-ack / invitations). Every returned protocol outcome of an op_id-carrying write rides in a 200 body (the canonical JsonEnvelope + receipt); non-2xx is reserved for transport/auth/integrity faults.

200.description: The login-flow grant (RFC-8628-shaped): the secret device_code to poll with (promoted to the SESSION's workspace-scoped bearer credential on approval), the human-facing user_code, and the BARE approval URL (the code never rides a URL). An invite_token in the body is recorded on the flow unvalidated — never a token oracle.

The poll status; `granted` carries the minted SESSION's credential (the promoted flow code), the session id + status, the joined workspace, and — when the flow carried an invitation naming one — the first-destination hint.
The all-outcome envelope + receipt (NEEDS_REVIEW on success — the candidate is committed without moving `current`).

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

200.description: The all-outcome envelope + receipt (OK / NEEDS_REVIEW / CONFLICT / DENIED …). An op_id retry replays byte-identically.

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

200.description: The all-outcome envelope + receipt — a revert is a FORWARD commit restoring the good version's bytes (the pointer never moves backward).

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

200.description: The all-outcome envelope + receipt (approve promotes; reject requires its reason; withdraw is author-only).

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

200.description: The self-end landed (no body sent): THIS credential's session is ended server-side and its reported state deleted with it. A retry answers the uniform 404 (already ended).

404.description: Missing/blank credential or an already-ended session (indistinguishable) — the caller treats this as already-signed-out.

200.description: The workspace channels (the structural `everyone` included), with the caller's membership marked.

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

200.description: The protect outcome (set, or a 200 DENIED REVIEWER_ROLE_REQUIRED / OWNER_ROLE_REQUIRED).

400.description: A level not valid for a channel (must be `curated` or `open`).

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

parameters.name: ch
parameters.description: The channel name (created on first placement, member-level self-serve).

parameters.name: skill
parameters.description: The skill's immutable id to place into the channel.

200.description: The curation outcome (placed / created, or a 200 DENIED CURATED_ROLE_REQUIRED / BAD_NAME / SKILL_NOT_ACTIVE).

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

parameters.name: skill
parameters.description: The skill's immutable id to remove from the channel.

200.description: The curation outcome (removed / not_placed, or a 200 DENIED CURATED_ROLE_REQUIRED).

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

200.description: This device's delivery answer (entitled skills, detached, notices, open-proposal count).

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

200.description: The invitation receipt — OK carries the InvitationData (address + invited + the honest mailed flag; the tokened invite link travels ONLY in the mail); a policy refusal is a 200 DENIED OWNER_ROLE_REQUIRED, an unresolvable hint a 200 DENIED UNKNOWN_SKILL / UNKNOWN_CHANNEL, an unarmed mail transport a 200 DENIED MAIL_NOT_CONFIGURED.

400.description: Malformed body or a malformed invitee email.

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

200.description: The caller's own membership (identity + address + role + inviter).

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

200.description: The notices were acked (idempotent — only the caller's own unacked rows move).

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

parameters.name: ch
parameters.description: The channel name to include in the caller's server-stored profile.

200.description: The all-outcome envelope: the profile row is present (idempotent).

404.description: Missing/blank credential, an ended session, or an unknown channel (indistinguishable).

parameters.name: ch
parameters.description: The channel name to drop from the caller's server-stored profile.

200.description: The all-outcome envelope; `data.status` mirrors the skill route's removal outcomes.

404.description: Missing/blank credential, an ended session, or an unknown channel (indistinguishable).

parameters.name: skill
parameters.description: The skill's immutable id to include in the caller's server-stored profile (the `-g` manifest layer). An optional JSON body `{"pin": "<64-hex>"}` pins the version.

200.description: The all-outcome envelope: the profile row is present (idempotent — re-including converges, a new pin replaces the old).

404.description: Missing/blank credential, an ended session, or an unknown skill (indistinguishable).

parameters.name: skill
parameters.description: The skill's immutable id to drop from the caller's server-stored profile.

200.description: The all-outcome envelope; `data.status` names HOW the removal settled — `removed` (the row is gone), `excluded` (the skill still arrives via a channel, so the profile records the negative stance instead), or `not_in_profile` (nothing to remove).

404.description: Missing/blank credential, an ended session, or an unknown skill (indistinguishable).

200.description: The review inbox: every OPEN proposal in the workspace, author message first.

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

204.description: The applied-state report was recorded (no body).

400.description: Malformed body or a bad skill / version id.

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

200.description: The workspace catalog (metadata only, no bytes; catalog visibility == membership).

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

parameters.name: object_id
parameters.description: The object's content id (64-char lowercase hex sha256).

200.description: The object's verified bytes (application/octet-stream). Served only through a skill that reaches it — never by bare hash.

404.description: Missing/blank credential, non-member, unreachable/unknown object, or a malformed id (indistinguishable).

200.description: The `current` pointer record (`ETag = "<generation>"`; a conditional GET answers 304).

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, non-member, or no pointer (indistinguishable).

parameters.name: skill
parameters.description: The skill's immutable id (an archived skill stays addressable).

200.description: The skill's version history (purge tombstones included) + its proposal events.

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

200.description: The skill's OPEN proposals — handles only (version id, base generation, opened-at); no bytes, no proposer.

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

200.description: The protect outcome (set, or a 200 DENIED REVIEWER_ROLE_REQUIRED / OWNER_ROLE_REQUIRED).

400.description: A level not valid for a skill (must be `reviewed` or `open`).

404.description: Missing/blank credential, unknown/revoked one, or non-member (indistinguishable).

200.description: The skill's audience (entitled members + their non-revoked devices).

404.description: Missing/blank credential, non-member, or unknown skill (indistinguishable).

parameters.name: version_id
parameters.description: The version's commit id (64-char lowercase hex).

200.description: The version's metadata + file listing (no blob bytes; the per-object read serves those).

404.description: Missing/blank credential, non-member, or an unknown/purged version (indistinguishable).

ActionCode.title: ActionCode
ActionCode.description: A machine-actionable next-action code. The values in `examples` are the known set; an unrecognized future code is still valid and MUST be executed via the action's `argv` (never rejected).

AddedNote.description:
The disclosure a `publish` attaches when it ADDED the skill to topos before shipping — the auto-add
convenience (`publish` accepts an untracked local source and adopts it first). Public disclosure only
(the same facts an explicit `topos add` would return); never a secret. **INFERRED** (additive-only).

harness_slug.description:
The harness registry slug the adopted directory was attributed to (e.g. `claude-code`), or `None`
for a plain directory adopted in place under no known harness.

name.description: The name the skill was adopted under (what `list` / `diff` / `publish` now resolve it by).

Affected.description: What an outcome refers to (every field optional — a policy flip has no skill/version).

version.description: A `version_id` (commit SHA-256, lowercase hex) when the outcome refers to a version.

version_id.description:
The full commit SHA-256 (lowercase hex) — the `version_id`. NOT the `bundle_digest`: the
pointer names the version, and the commit transitively pins the bytes.

DeviceAuthHint.description:
The first-destination HINT an accepted invitation named — decorated onto a `granted` poll (and
the direct-accept answer) so the enrolled client's post-accept subscribe can target it. `kind`
is the bundle catalog's own tag (`skill` today) or the literal `channel` — displayed and routed
on, never trusted as authority.

kind.description: What the hinted thing is: the catalog's `kind` tag, or `channel`.

name.description: The hinted bundle's or channel's name in the joined workspace.

DeviceAuthPollRequest.description: `POST /v1/device/token` body — poll a device-authorization flow for its outcome.

DeviceAuthPollResponse.description:
`POST /v1/device/token` response — the poll `status`; a `granted` poll carries the device's ONE
bearer credential (the promoted device code — returned here and stored from this ONE field),
its device id, and the joined workspace. A re-poll of an approved flow returns the same answer.

credential.description:
The device's plaintext bearer credential — present ONLY when `status` is `granted`. Returned
once per poll; the server stores only its sha256.

device_id.description: The registered device's id — present ONLY when `status` is `granted`.

oneOf.description:
The invitation's first-destination hint — present only on a `granted` poll whose flow
carried an invite token naming one.

link_status.description:
The FIRST device↔workspace link's born status — present ONLY when `status` is `granted`:
`"active"`, or `"pending"` when the workspace's device-approval knob gated it (approval
mints the registration and the first link together server-side). Absent on an older
producer ⇒ treat as `"active"`.

session_id.description:
The minted SESSION's id — the session-model login wire's grant half (a session = user ×
workspace × installation; the credential is workspace-scoped). Present ONLY when `status`
is `granted` on a session-serving producer. **Additive.**

session_status.description:
The session's born status — `"active"`, or `"pending"` while the workspace's
session-approval knob holds it. Absent ⇒ treat as active. **Additive.**

oneOf.description: The joined workspace — present ONLY when `status` is `granted`.

DeviceAuthPollStatus.description:
A device-authorization poll status (snake_case). `granted` carries the credential + the joined
workspace; every other status carries only itself.

DeviceAuthStartRequest.description:
`POST /v1/device/authorize` body — begin a device-authorization flow toward a workspace named by
its address slug. Whether the name exists is never disclosed on this route: an unknown name runs
the same flow to the same uniform denial.

invite_token.description:
The invitation-link token a `follow <invite-url>` enrollment carries. Recorded on the flow
(as its hash) UNVALIDATED — this unauthenticated start is never a token oracle; the approval
ceremony resolves it and weaves the invitation accept into its own fence.

requested_name.description:
A human-readable device name shown on the approval page (a confused-deputy guard, not
authority) and kept as the device's display name once approved.

workspace.description:
The workspace ADDRESS slug the device asks to join (`topos.sh/<name>` minus the origin). An
EMPTY string names "the workspace the origin itself addresses" (single-tenant installs, where
the origin IS its one workspace); a non-empty value is the address slug as today.

device_code.description:
The SECRET device code the client polls `device/token` with. On approval this same secret is
promoted to the device's bearer credential (see the module note), so it is stored like one.

interval_secs.description: The minimum poll interval, in seconds.

user_code.description:
The short human-facing code the approval page displays (a cross-check, never typed as a
secret).

verification_uri.description:
The approval URL a signed-in human visits. The code NEVER rides a URL: the client prints
this bare address and the short code on separate lines, and the page's lookup is a POST.

DeviceAuthWorkspace.description:
The workspace context a `granted` poll carries — everything the CLI needs to record what it
enrolled into (the id it scopes requests by, the address slug it joined at, and a display name).

name.description: The workspace's ADDRESS slug (what the human typed at `follow`).

workspace_id.description: The workspace id (the `{ws}` path segment of every subsequent request).

InvitationData.description:
`POST /v1/workspaces/{ws}/invitations` success `data` — what the inviter pastes onward: the
workspace ADDRESS. Deliberately NOT the tokened link: the link travels only in the invitation
mail, so the inviter's receipt can never stand in for the invitee's mailbox.

address.description: The workspace address (the share link — it carries nothing; the roster is the lock).

invited.description: The emails now seated as invited members (canonical form).

mailed.description: Whether invitation mail was sent server-side (`true` only when the server can send mail).

InvitationRequest.description:
`POST /v1/workspaces/{ws}/invitations` body — invitation as an INVITATION-ROW write: each email
becomes a pending 7-day claim redeemable through the single-use link the server MAILS (the token
never appears in this exchange — the mailbox is its one channel). At most ONE optional
first-destination hint (a skill or a channel of this workspace) rides the invitation and is
delivered by the accept. No role field — every CLI invitee starts as a member (roles are raised
later, on the web). Owner-only: only a workspace owner may send (and revoke) invitations.

channel.description:
The first-destination CHANNEL hint (a channel name in this workspace). At most one of
`skill`/`channel` may be set.

emails.description: The emails to seat as invited members (folded to the canonical lowercase form server-side).

skill.description:
The first-destination SKILL hint (a bundle name in this workspace). At most one of
`skill`/`channel` may be set.

JsonEnvelope.description:
The one common envelope every verb emits. Never prompts; prose (TTY) is rendered from the
SAME typed value where practical.

next_actions.description: Stable, machine-actionable next steps — each carries a complete argv.

oneOf.description: The durable idempotency record — present on **every** terminal op, including failures.

schema_version.description: Always `1` for this contract version (the schema pins it `const`).

NextAction.description:
A machine-actionable next step. The `argv` is the ready-to-exec command; `code` lets an agent
branch on the known set and still pass through unknowns. The three safety fields are ADDITIVE
and optional (absent = unknown) — a producer fills them from its one rules module, never
per call site, so the classification cannot drift between surfaces.

argv.description: A complete argv array — execute as-is (no TTY parsing).

mutates.description:
Whether executing `argv` CHANGES state — local files (skill bytes, the sidecar) or shared
workspace state. Absent = unknown (e.g. a bare `RETRY`, whose safety is the retried
command's own).

needs.description:
The placeholder names the `argv` template still needs filled before it can execute — one
entry per distinct `<name>` token part (e.g. `"workspace-address"` for an argv carrying
`<workspace-address>`). Empty/absent = the argv is complete and executable as-is. ADDITIVE.

needs_network.description: Whether executing `argv` dials the plane. Absent = unknown.

risk_note.description:
A one-line caution for an action whose effect a human/agent should weigh before running
(team-visible moves, local discards). Absent = nothing to flag.

NoticeAckRequest.description:
`POST /v1/workspaces/{ws}/notices/ack` body — acknowledge notices by id (person-scoped read-state;
the silent hook fetches without acking, an interactive narration acks what it narrated).

ids.description: The notice ids to mark read (only the caller's own unacked rows move; unknown ids are ignored).

ProposeData.description:
`publish --propose` (opens a PR; uploads a full candidate **without moving `current`**). Returns
`NEEDS_REVIEW`. **INFERRED.**

oneOf.description:
Present ONLY when THIS `publish --propose` invocation ADDED the skill to topos first (the auto-add
convenience — a proposal of an untracked local source adopts it before opening the PR). Discloses the
one local `add` the propose folded in; `None` when the skill was already tracked. **INFERRED.**

ProposeRequest.description:
`POST /v1/proposals` body — opens a proposal (a PR): ingests a full candidate **without moving
`current`** (`NEEDS_REVIEW`). The authority's `propose` op takes the **same** input shape as
`publish` (candidate + device + `op_id` + `expected`); there is **no** separate title/body on the op (a
title/body, if ever surfaced, would be composed into the candidate's commit message).

candidate.description: The full candidate bundle to ingest as the proposal's content.

channel.description:
The `--to` channel placement (same semantics as `PublishRequest.channel`): the placement
applies when the proposal opens — a placement is curation, gated by the channel's mode,
independent of the version's review.

display_name.description:
The skill's human display name (the author's skill-folder name) — ADVISORY metadata, carried for
symmetry with [`PublishRequest`]. It rides the proposal but is never digested or part of the
candidate; the plane records a name only when the pointer actually moves (a later approve/publish).

expected.description: The generation the proposal is born against (its base); a stale base later makes it non-current.

op_id.description: The client-minted UUIDv4 idempotency key (replays the stored receipt on retry).

skill_id.description: The target skill id within the workspace.

ProtectionSetRequest.description:
`PUT /v1/workspaces/{ws}/skills/{skill}/protection` and
`PUT /v1/workspaces/{ws}/channels/{ch}/protection` body — the safety knob. Levels are per kind:
`reviewed`/`open` for a skill bundle, `curated`/`open` for a channel. Tightening takes reviewer+;
loosening back to `open` takes an owner.

level.description: The target level (`open`, `reviewed` for skills, `curated` for channels).

PublishData.description:
`publish` (a direct publish that moves `current`). Under a `reviewed` bundle a direct publish is
DOWNGRADED to a proposal (see [`ProposeData`]); an un-enrolled publish is refused typed (enroll with
`topos follow <workspace-address>` first). **INFERRED.**

oneOf.description:
Present ONLY when THIS `publish` invocation ADDED the skill to topos first (the auto-add
convenience: `publish <name>|<name>@<harness>|<dir>` adopts an untracked LOCAL skill, then ships it
in one command). Discloses the one local `add` the publish folded in; `None` when the skill was
already tracked.

bundle_digest.description:
The byte-exact digest of the shipped bytes — computed over the draft before any network call;
an optional `<skill>@<digest>` pin gates it.

converted_from.description:
The local-path spelling the manifest carried BEFORE the transfer (the inverse is
`topos add <converted_from>` after a `topos remove <reference>`). **INFERRED**
(additive-only).

current_generation.description: The pointer's new generation after the move.

invite_line.description:
The paste-able teammate handoff line (`Ask your agent: …`) — the join instruction that
brings a teammate's machine into the workspace, composed from the workspace's server
origin + address. Absent when the address is not known (a best-effort read — the publish
itself is unaffected). **INFERRED** (additive-only).

manifest.description:
The GOVERNANCE-TRANSFER receipt half: the manifest whose local-path line this publish
rewrote to the governed workspace reference. Absent when no manifest referenced the bundle
by path (an already-governed republish). **INFERRED** (additive-only).

name.description:
The skill's NAME — the handle humans speak and the TTY success line leads with
(`Published <name>@…`); the opaque `skill_id` above stays the machine key.

placement_withheld.description:
The channel whose placement was WITHHELD by its curated mode (the receipt's
`details.placement = curated_role_required`): the publish itself landed — catalog + moved
pointer — but the skill's reference was NOT placed into this channel (the `--to` target, or
the default `everyone` for a brand-new skill). A reviewer or owner places it
(`topos channel add <channel> <skill>`). **INFERRED** (additive-only).

reference.description: The canonical workspace reference the manifest now stores. **INFERRED** (additive-only).

PublishRequest.description:
`POST /v1/publish` body — a direct publish that moves `current`. The acting device rides the
`Authorization: Bearer` workspace credential (resolved in-transaction to its registry row + the
membership gate); the server stamps `created_at`.
Under `review-required` the authority refuses this closed with `APPROVAL_REQUIRED`, ingesting nothing.

candidate.description: The full candidate bundle to ingest + publish.

channel.description:
The `--to` channel placement: place the skill's reference into this channel (created on first
use, member-level; a `curated` channel needs reviewer+ — the placement outcome rides the
receipt's details, independently of the version gate). Absent ⇒ no explicit placement (a
brand-new skill's reference still lands in `everyone` when its mode admits the caller; a
curated `everyone` withholds a member's default placement, disclosed on the receipt — the
publish itself lands catalog-only).

display_name.description:
The skill's human display name (the author's skill-folder name) — ADVISORY metadata the plane
stores last-writer-wins and serves for display only (a follower names its folder by it; the
dashboard shows it). It is NOT part of the byte-exact bundle digest, the candidate, or the commit
id — a rename never changes a version id or digest. Absent ⇒ the plane keeps any existing name
(never clobbered to NULL).

expected.description: The generation this publish's compare-and-set targets; a stale value yields `CONFLICT`.

op_id.description: The client-minted UUIDv4 idempotency key — the same `op_id` replays the stored receipt byte-for-byte.

skill_id.description: The target skill id within the workspace.

oneOf.description:
The external-origin provenance this publish carries to the server (a bundle imported from
GitHub remembers its parent — the fork-that-remembers model). Absent for ordinary local
authorship. **Additive.**

workspace_id.description: The target workspace id (the receipt + pointer scope).

Receipt.description:
The ONE canonical receipt across all outcomes — the durable idempotency record (present even
on failures): one stable receipt per op, identical on retry.

created_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp (the plane stamps it; never an ambient clock in `topos-core`).

op_id.description: Client-minted UUIDv4, persisted before the first send.

schema_version.description: Always `1` for this contract version (the schema pins it `const`).

RevertData.description:
`revert` (a **forward** git-revert restoring older bytes as a new, higher-generation version —
never a pointer rollback, never a delete). `--to` names the GOOD version. **INFERRED.**

name.description:
The skill's NAME — the handle humans speak and the TTY success line leads with
(`Reverted <name> …`); the opaque `skill_id` above stays the machine key.

new_version_id.description: The new forward-revert commit that carries those bytes.

reverted_to.description: The good version named by `--to` (the bytes being restored).

RevertRequest.description:
`POST /v1/reverts` body — a **forward** revert: the server constructs a new 1-parent commit carrying the
`good` version's bytes on top of `current` (`seq` advances; the pointer never moves backward). There is
**no candidate** — the server reads `good`'s tree + digest from its provenance and builds the commit.

author.description: The author device id recorded in the forward-revert commit frame.

expected.description: The generation this revert's compare-and-set targets; a stale value yields `CONFLICT`.

good.description: The GOOD version (the `version_id` whose bytes are restored) as 64-char lowercase hex.

op_id.description: The client-minted UUIDv4 idempotency key (replays the stored receipt on retry).

skill_id.description: The target skill id within the workspace.

ReviewData.description:
`review` (`--approve` / `--reject` a proposal). Approve is a compare-and-set on the base; a stale
base returns `CONFLICT`. **INFERRED.**

current_generation.description: The pointer's new generation when an approval moved `current`.

ReviewDecision.description:
A review verdict — `approve` promotes, `reject` carries a reason back, `withdraw` is the author
retracting their own open proposal. **INFERRED.**

ReviewRequest.description:
`POST /v1/reviews` body — a governance decision on an open proposal. `approve` runs the shared
generation compare-and-set on the proposal's base (a stale base ⇒ `CONFLICT`) and, under
`review_required`, enforces four-eyes (the proposer may not self-approve) before promoting; `reject`
is a standalone status flip (no pointer move).

decision.description:
The verdict — `approve` (promote), `reject` (with its reason), or `withdraw` (the author
retracting their own open proposal).

expected.description:
The generation the approval's compare-and-set targets (the proposal's base); a stale value on
an `approve` yields `CONFLICT`.

op_id.description: The client-minted UUIDv4 idempotency key (replays the stored receipt on retry).

proposal.description: The proposal being reviewed, named by its candidate commit id (64-char lowercase hex).

reason.description: The reject's reason, carried back to the author (REQUIRED on `reject`; absent otherwise).

skill_id.description: The target skill id within the workspace.

TerminalOutcome.description: The closed set of terminal outcomes the agent branches on. SCREAMING_SNAKE on the wire.

WireAppliedReport.description:
`PUT /v1/workspaces/{ws}/report` body — the fleet's applied-state report: this device's
`(skill, applied version)` snapshot after a reconcile. The plane upserts the snapshot, drops the
non-detached rows it no longer names, and stamps the staleness clock (last-writer-wins; no receipt).
The acting device rides the `Authorization: Bearer` workspace credential, never a body field.

applied.description: The device's applied skills (possibly empty — a device holding nothing reports an empty list).

schema_version.description: Always `1` for this contract version (the schema pins it `const`).

WireAppliedSkill.description:
One applied-state row a device reports: the skill and the version it currently holds after its
reconcile.

version_id.description: The version this device holds (64-char lowercase hex).

WireCandidate.description:
A full candidate bundle: every file's bytes, the declared parents, and the author + message — the
shared input the `publish` and `propose` writes ingest (the `revert` write needs no candidate; the
server builds that forward commit from the good version). Maps to `plane-store`'s
`CandidateUpload { files, parents, author, message }` — each `parents` entry hex-decoded into a
`CommitId`.

author.description: The author device id recorded in the commit frame.

files.description: Every file in the candidate bundle (each server-rehashed).

message.description: The commit message (title + body composed into one string).

parents.description:
The candidate commit's declared parents, each a 64-char lowercase-hex `version_id` (`0` parents for a
genesis publish, `1` for a normal publish / propose, `2` for an author merge). Each must already be
present in the workspace; a lie changes the recomputed commit id, so the server need not trust it.

WireChannelEntry.description: One channel in the workspace's channel index.

builtin.description: Whether this is the structural `everyone` (roster-derived membership; cannot be joined or left).

member.description: Whether the CALLER is a member (always `true` on `everyone`).

member_count.description: How many people the channel reaches (confirmed roster size for `everyone`).

WireChannelIndex.description:
`GET /v1/workspaces/{ws}/channels` response — the workspace's channels with the caller's own
membership marked. Member-scoped.

WireChannelSkill.description: One skill reference inside a channel entry.

WireCurrentRecord.description:
The `current` pointer's wire body — the UNSIGNED document the pointer read serves and OK
receipts embed. It is unsigned: authority is the database row behind the pointer, and integrity
is the content-addressed `version_id` re-verified byte-for-byte on apply. A versioned envelope;
`ETag = "<generation>"`.

schema_version.description: Always `1` for this contract version (the schema pins it `const`).

WireDelivery.description:
`GET /v1/workspaces/{ws}/delivery` response body — the update answer for ONE enrolled device: the
entitled skills (what this device should have), the person's detached skills (freeze-in-place, never
cleaned), the unacked notices feed, and the open-proposal count across the entitled set. The
session-start hook fetches it once per workspace and reconciles the harness against it, silently. A
versioned envelope.

detached.description:
The skill ids the person detached (unfollowed, or lapsed via a channel leave / removal) and that are
NOT currently re-entitled — every device freezes these in place, never cleaning them.
RETIRED on the session wire (a current server never sends it); defaulted for the transition.

excluded.description:
The skill ids THIS DEVICE excludes ("not on this device") — the third actor in the who-acts
split, alongside the person (`detached`) and upstream (absent from `skills` entirely). The copy
leaves this device; the person keeps receiving it on every other device; `follow` here lifts it.
Sent so the client narrates the true CAUSE instead of mistaking an exclusion written elsewhere
(the web, a second tool) for an upstream withdrawal.

link_status.description:
RETIRED spelling of [`Self::session_status`] (the device-link wire) — read as a fallback,
never produced by a current server.

notices.description: The unacked, person-scoped notices (verdicts, proposal closures, …).

proposals_awaiting.description: The count of OPEN, non-stale proposals across the entitled skills (the review-inbox pressure gauge).

schema_version.description: Always `1` for this contract version (the schema pins it `const`).

session_status.description:
THIS session's standing — `"active"` or `"pending"`. A `"pending"` delivery carries empty
`skills` / `notices` and `proposals_awaiting: 0` — no data flows over a pending session;
the client skips the workspace quietly and `topos status` shows the wait. Serde-defaulted
only so the retired `link_status` spelling still parses during the wire transition.

skills.description: The entitled skills — everything this device should have (a possibly-empty list).

staleness_window_ms.description:
The workspace's staleness window (epoch milliseconds) — the ONE clock the fleet page and the
client's hook warning both read: a device whose last report is older than this is stale.
Additive: an older producer that omits it falls back to the one-week default (a whole delivery
body must never fail to parse over one new field).

workspace_id.description: The workspace this delivery is scoped to (echoed from the path).

WireDeliverySkill.description:
One skill THIS device should have, in the delivery answer: the catalog identity, the pinned `current`
version + its consent digest, the resolved protection posture, and the `via` attribution. **NO bytes** —
after reconciling the client fetches each version through the per-blob bundle read.

bundle_digest.description: The `current` byte-exact consent hash (64-char lowercase hex) — the fetch + re-hash pin.

display_name.description: The unsigned, advisory display name (the author's folder name); absent ⇒ show `name`.

kind.description:
The catalog's bundle kind — `"skill"` for everything that exists today. Display metadata
only: clients render it and never branch on it (an OPEN vocabulary, like `protection`).
Additive: an older producer that omits it is serving skills.

name.description: The catalog's user-facing name (the on-disk directory name for a fresh install).

protection.description:
The resolved per-bundle protection posture — `"open"` or `"reviewed"` (the client's publish
preflight; the server re-decides authoritatively on every write). An OPEN string for forward
compat — a client treats an unrecognized value as the stricter posture.

version_id.description: The pinned `current` version's commit id (64-char lowercase hex).

via.description: Why this device is entitled to the skill (channels ∪ direct).

WireError.description:
The flat wire error (rich `thiserror` enums stay internal). Carries a
stable code + retryability + the safe next actions; never raw SQL/git strings.

code.description:
A stable, machine-branchable error code, distinct from (and finer than) `outcome`. This is an
**open, additive** string vocabulary — it is intentionally NOT a closed enum: new codes are
added over time, and a consumer must treat an unrecognized `code` as the `outcome` it carries.

context.description: Structured context (no TTY prose to parse).

WireFile.description:
One file of a candidate bundle, uploaded **by value**. The raw bytes ride as base64 in the JSON body
(`content_base64`, standard alphabet); the server base64-decodes them and **rehashes every byte** to
derive the content id — there is no reference-by-id, and a client hash is never trusted. Maps to
`plane-store`'s `UploadedFile { path, mode, bytes }` (decode `content_base64` → `bytes`).

content_base64.description: The raw file bytes, base64-encoded. The server decodes then rehashes them (server-side digest).

WireFileMode.description:
A candidate file's mode on the wire — the two git regular-file modes as their literal octal strings
(`"100644"` / `"100755"`). A **closed** wire mirror of `topos_core::digest::FileMode`: that kernel enum
lives in a `no_std` crate `topos-types` does not depend on (and it carries no serde/schema derives), so
the wire leaf owns its own copy. The route handler maps it 1:1 at the edge —
`Regular ⇔ FileMode::Regular`, `Executable ⇔ FileMode::Executable` — for both the inbound candidate
([`WireFile`]) and the outbound version metadata ([`WireVersionFile`]).

WireLogProposal.description: One proposal event in a skill's history (open, and every resolution).

created_at.description: When the proposal was opened (server-stamped string).

resolved_at.description: When it was resolved (server-stamped string), when resolved.

resolved_reason.description: The resolution reason (a reject's rationale; a closure's cause), when recorded.

version_id.description: The proposed version's commit id (64-char lowercase hex).

WireLogVersion.description:
One version in a skill's history. A PURGED version stays listed — who purged it and when — with its
bytes gone (author/message may be absent once the underlying objects are reclaimed).

author.description: The version author, when the commit object is still readable.

current.description: Whether this version is the skill's `current`.

message.description: The version message, when the commit object is still readable.

purged_at.description: When the version's bytes were purged (epoch milliseconds) — the tombstone half.

purged_by.description: Who purged the version's bytes — the tombstone half.

version_id.description: The version's commit id (64-char lowercase hex).

WireMe.description:
`GET /v1/workspaces/{ws}/me` response — the caller's own membership describe: who you are here,
who added you, and the workspace's address block. Member-scoped (the uniform not-found otherwise).

address.description: The workspace's full address (the share link — server-built on the public link base).

invited_by.description: Who invited this principal (absent for a genesis owner).

link_status.description:
THIS device's link to the workspace — `"active"` or `"pending"` (a pending link awaits an
owner's approval; no skill data flows over it). Serde-defaulted to `"active"` for schema
stability: a producer predating device links serves only active-equivalent access.

WireNotice.description:
One unacked, person-scoped notice in the delivery feed. `kind` is an OPEN vocabulary that grows without
a schema break (today's values include `"verdict"` and `"proposal_closed"`); every other field is
present only when the notice names it. The silent auto-update hook fetches these without acking; an
interactive surface acks by id.

actor.description: The actor whose action raised the notice, when recorded.

created_at.description: When the notice was created (the server-stamped RFC-3339 string).

kind.description:
The notice kind — an OPEN vocabulary (e.g. `"verdict"`, `"proposal_closed"`); a client ignores a
kind it does not recognize.

message.description: A rendered human message, when the notice carries one.

outcome.description: The outcome (e.g. a verdict's `approve` / `reject`), when recorded.

reason.description: The human reason (a review's rationale), when recorded.

skill_id.description: The skill the notice concerns, when it names one.

skill_name.description: The skill's current catalog name (joined for narration), when the notice names a skill.

version_id.description: The version the notice concerns (64-char lowercase hex), when it names one.

WireOpenProposal.description:
One OPEN proposal on the wire — its candidate `version_id` (the `@hash`), the `base_generation` it was
opened against, and when. The proposals-listing read returns ONLY these three fields: **no bytes, no
proposer, no roles, no rendered diff**. Mirrors `plane-store`'s `OpenProposalSummary` with the id hex-encoded.

base_generation.description:
The generation the proposal was opened against (its base); when `current` advances past it
the proposal stales and drops out of this list.

created_at.description: When the proposal was opened (the server-stamped RFC-3339 string).

version_id.description: The proposal's candidate commit id (64-char lowercase hex) — the `<skill>@<version_id>` handle.

WireProposalEntry.description: One open proposal in the workspace's review inbox.

base_version_id.description: The base version the proposal was built on (64-char lowercase hex).

created_at.description: When the proposal was opened (the server-stamped string).

message.description: The author's message (the proposed version's commit message) — what a reviewer reads FIRST.

stale.description: Whether `current` has moved since the proposal's base (a stale proposal needs a re-propose).

version_id.description: The proposed version's commit id (64-char lowercase hex) — the review target handle.

yours.description:
Whether the CALLER authored this proposal — the server computes it from the resolved user id
(never email equality). The client's inbox uses it to split the outbox (yours) from the inbox
(others') and to offer the author `--withdraw` instead of `--approve`. `Some` is AUTHORITATIVE
either way (a served `false` is a served "not yours" — never overridden client-side); only a
producer predating the field omits it (`None`), and only then does the client fall back to
comparing principals.

WireProposalIndex.description:
`GET /v1/workspaces/{ws}/proposals` response — every OPEN proposal in the workspace, author-message
first. The caller splits inbox (others') from outbox (own) by `proposer`. Member-scoped.

WireProposalList.description:
`GET /v1/workspaces/{ws}/skills/{skill}/proposals` response body — the OPEN, non-stale proposals on a
rostered skill (a possibly-empty list, ordered by `(created_at, version_id)`). A staled proposal is absent
(keep == read == list); the list carries no bytes and no proposer — it is the thin handle a client turns
into a `diff` / `review` follow-up.

WireProtocolCard.description:
The MACHINE face of the constant protocol card — what an `Accept: application/json` GET of any
resource address returns, identical for every path (no content, no existence signal): just enough
for a client to re-root onto the API and run `follow`.

api_base_url.description: The API base URL the client re-roots onto (the origin serving `/v1`).

card.description: The constant discriminant a client dispatches on (`"topos-protocol-card"`).

schema_version.description: Always `1` for this contract version (the schema pins it `const`).

WireReach.description:
`GET /v1/workspaces/{ws}/skills/{skill}/reach` response — the audience a change to this skill
reaches (the describe number behind `publish` and `protect`). Member-scoped.

devices.description: How many registered, non-revoked devices those people hold here.

persons.description: How many people are entitled to the skill.

WireSkillIndex.description:
`GET /v1/workspaces/{ws}/skills` response body — the workspace catalog (every skill holding a `current`),
authorized by workspace membership (the `Authorization: Bearer` workspace credential names the
device; catalog
visibility == membership, on both cloud and self-host). Metadata only, no bytes; a possibly-empty list
ordered by `skill_id`.

WireSkillIndexEntry.description:
One skill of the workspace catalog, as `GET /v1/workspaces/{ws}/skills` returns it: the discovery
metadata a member browses to decide what to follow — **NO bytes**. Mirrors `plane-store`'s
`SkillIndexRow` with every 32-byte id hex-encoded.

bundle_digest.description: The `current` byte-exact consent hash (64-char lowercase hex).

display_name.description: The unsigned, advisory folder display name (may be absent).

kind.description:
The catalog's bundle kind — `"skill"` for everything that exists today. Display metadata
only: clients render it and never branch on it (an OPEN vocabulary, like `status`).
Additive: an older producer that omits it is serving skills.

name.description: The catalog's user-facing name (a pre-catalog seeded pointer falls back to the skill id).

open_proposals.description: The count of OPEN, non-stale proposals on the skill.

status.description:
The catalog lifecycle status — `"active"` / `"archived"` (a deleted skill has no `current` row and
so no entry). An OPEN string, deliberately: a new state can land without a schema break.

version_id.description: The `current` version's commit id (64-char lowercase hex).

WireSkillLog.description:
`GET /v1/workspaces/{ws}/skills/{skill}/log` response — the skill's version history (purge
tombstones included) and its proposal events. An ARCHIVED skill stays addressable here; asking by a
freed base name answers the archived successor as a hint. Member-scoped.

base_name.description: The pre-archive name, when archived (what the skill was called before the rename freed it).

kind.description:
The catalog's bundle kind — `"skill"` for everything that exists today. Display metadata
only: clients render it and never branch on it. Additive: an older producer omits it.

name.description: The skill's current catalog name (the archived spelling once archived).

versions.description:
The versions, newest first where orderable (the ancestry walk stops at reclaimed history;
tombstoned leftovers follow unordered).

WireUpstream.description:
The upstream-provenance block a publish may carry: where the bundle's bytes originally came
from (`host`/`repo`, the in-repo `path`, the imported `commit`, a recorded license). The server
records it as the bundle's upstream link + the version's upstream commit.

commit.description: The upstream commit the imported bytes came from, when known.

host.description: The origin host (`github.com` — the only recognized value today).

license.description: A license identifier/filename recorded at import time, when found.

path.description: The bundle's path within the repo ("" / absent = the repo root).

WireVersionFile.description:
One file of a version's metadata on the wire — its path, mode, and content id (`object_id`), mirroring
`plane-store`'s `VersionFile` with the id hex-encoded. The **bytes are NOT here**: a client fetches each
by `object_id` through the bundle (object) read route.

object_id.description: The file's content id (64-char lowercase hex) — the handle the per-blob read route resolves.

WireVersionMeta.description:
`GET /v1/workspaces/{ws}/skills/{skill}/versions/{version_id}` response body — a version's authenticated
metadata: its id, the COMPLETE parent set, display author + message, the consent `bundle_digest`, and the
per-file `(path, mode, object_id)` leaves. Mirrors `plane-store`'s `VersionMeta` with every 32-byte id
hex-encoded. Assembled WITHOUT reading any blob bytes; the `bundle_digest` is the pin the client's
per-blob fetches + its own re-hash must reproduce.

author.description: The author device id from the commit frame.

bundle_digest.description: The byte-exact consent hash over the bundle (64-char lowercase hex) — the fetch + re-hash pin.

files.description: The per-file leaves, in the version's recorded order.

message.description: The commit message (title + body as one string).

parents.description:
The COMPLETE parent set, each a 64-char lowercase-hex commit id (`0` for genesis, `1` normally, `2`
for an author merge).

version_id.description: This version's commit id (64-char lowercase hex).

WireVia.description:
Why THIS device is entitled to a delivered skill — the attribution the client's narration reads. A
delivered skill always has at least one of the two: it rides one or more channels, and/or the person
follows it directly (a direct follow survives every channel drop).

channels.description: The channels delivering the skill (names, sorted; `everyone` is present when it delivers).

direct.description: Whether the person also follows the skill directly (independent of any channel).

tags.name: writes
tags.description: Session-credential writes (publish / propose / revert / review) and the member-lane row ops (channel curation / protection / notices-ack).

tags.name: reads
tags.description: Session-credential reads (current / bundles / versions / proposals / catalog / delivery / me / channels / log / reach) plus the body-light applied-state report.

tags.name: rows
tags.description: The server-stored profile row ops (the person's `-g` manifest layer).

tags.name: enrollment
tags.description: The RFC-8628-shaped login flow (start / poll; approval promotes the flow code to the SESSION's workspace-scoped bearer credential) and the session self-end.
